text: This exercise is ring roller coaster one. It is one of several ring roller coaster exercises featured in our curriculum and focuses primarily on robotic wrist articulation. The exercise requires the two rings travel around a metal pipe from one end to the other following a tortuous course. In our experience, we have found that the best way to quickly move the rings is by using only the right hand, as it allows for maximal range of motion while simultaneously moving the ring in the right to left direction.

The surgeon uses the robotic grasper to manipulate the ring along the metal pipe, alternating between pronation and supination of the hand. The ring travels over horizontal and upper curves with the hand supinated, and pronated while moving upwards. The same motions are repeated for the second ring, demonstrating precise wrist articulation.

Keys to scoring high on this exercise require minimizing touching the pipe and excessive force, avoiding dropping the rings, maintaining good vision, and moving efficiently through the course as time is against you.
